history.qwen_observation: The Kotlin Maven Plugin 1.8.10 does not support JVM target 21, causing compilation to fail during the java21_transforms step.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading the Kotlin Maven Plugin to 1.9.24 (which fully supports Java 21) before changing the build target to Java 21 will resolve the 'Unknown JVM target version: 21' error.

history.qwen_observation: EqualsVerifier tests fail on JDK 21 because the project uses EqualsVerifier 3.14.1, which bundles a Byte Buddy version that does not support Java 21 bytecode.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading EqualsVerifier to 3.15.6+ pulls in a Byte Buddy version with official Java 21 support, resolving the IllegalArgumentException thrown during test execution on the new JDK.

history.qwen_observation: EqualsVerifier 3.15.6 depends on a Byte Buddy version that lacks official Java 21 support, causing IllegalArgumentException during test execution.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading EqualsVerifier to 3.17.0 pulls in a newer Byte Buddy release that officially supports Java 21 bytecode, eliminating the need for experimental VM flags and resolving the test regressions.

history.qwen_observation: EqualsVerifier tests fail on JDK 21 because the bundled Byte Buddy version only supports up to Java 20 (class file version 64), throwing IllegalArgumentException for Java 21 (version 65).
history.qwen_rationale: Forcing the Byte Buddy dependency to version 1.15.10, which officially supports Java 21 class files, will resolve the IllegalArgumentException without requiring VM property flags or further EqualsVerifier version bumps.
